name: Oud
description: Also known as agarwood, oud is a prized resinous heartwood formed in Aquilaria trees. It produces a complex, dark, woody aroma that is the cornerstone of Arabian perfumery.

name: Rose
description: The queen of flowers in perfumery. Varieties include Turkish, Bulgarian, and Taif rose, each with distinct character.

name: Vanilla
description: Extracted from vanilla orchid pods. Adds warmth, sweetness, and depth.

name: Sandalwood
description: Creamy, soft, warm wood note. Indian sandalwood (Mysore) is the most prized variety.

name: Amber
description: A warm, resinous accord typically created from labdanum, benzoin, and vanilla.

name: Musk
description: Originally from musk deer, now synthetic. Provides warmth, sensuality, and skin-like closeness.

name: Bergamot
description: A bright, slightly bitter citrus from southern Italy. The most used top note in perfumery.

name: Saffron
description: The world's most expensive spice adds a leathery, honeyed, metallic warmth.

name: Incense
description: Smoky, resinous, spiritual. Evokes Middle Eastern rituals and sacred spaces.

name: Cedar
description: Dry, woody, pencil-shaving character. Atlas and Virginia cedar are common varieties.

name: Tonka Bean
description: Sweet, warm, almond-vanilla character with a hint of tobacco.

name: Patchouli
description: Dark, earthy, slightly sweet. A base note staple.

name: Jasmine
description: Rich, sweet, intensely floral. Jasmine sambac and grandiflorum are key varieties.

name: Vetiver
description: Earthy, smoky, green-woody. Haitian vetiver is the most sought after.

name: Cinnamon
description: Warm, sweet spice. Adds instant warmth and a gourmand-spicy facet.

name: Cardamom
description: Fresh, aromatic, slightly sweet spice.

name: Praline
description: Caramelized sugar and nuts. Adds a sweet, comforting quality.

name: Benzoin
description: Sweet, balsamic resin with vanilla-like warmth.

name: Labdanum
description: Rich, dark, slightly sweet resin. A key component of amber accords.

name: Turkish Rose
description: Richer and more honeyed than Bulgarian rose. A staple of Arabian perfumery.

name: Pepper
description: Black or pink pepper adds a sharp, spicy opening.

name: Nutmeg
description: Warm, slightly sweet, aromatic spice.

name: Lemon
description: Bright, sharp, clean citrus.

name: Orange Blossom
description: Sweet, honeyed, slightly green floral.

name: Iris
description: Powdery, elegant, buttery-violet.

name: Tobacco
description: Warm, sweet, slightly smoky.

name: Leather
description: Smoky, animalic, dry.

name: Frankincense
description: Also known as olibanum. Resinous, citrusy, slightly spicy.

name: Myrrh
description: Sweet, resinous, slightly medicinal.

name: Coconut
description: Tropical, creamy, sweet.

name: Date
description: Sweet, rich, fruity. A distinctly Arabian note.

name: Fig
description: Green, milky, slightly sweet.

name: Lavender
description: Aromatic, herbaceous, slightly sweet.

name: Geranium
description: Rose-like but greener and mintier.

name: Ylang Ylang
description: Sweet, exotic, tropical floral.

name: Tuberose
description: Intensely sweet, creamy, opulent white floral.

name: Clove
description: Warm, spicy, slightly sweet.

name: Ginger
description: Fresh, spicy, slightly sweet.

name: Apple
description: Crisp, sweet, fresh fruit note.

name: Raspberry
description: Sweet, tart, berry note.

name: Peach
description: Soft, sweet, juicy stone fruit.

name: Plum
description: Dark, rich, sweet fruit.

name: Taif Rose
description: The most prized rose in Arabian perfumery, grown only in Saudi Arabia.

name: Suede
description: Soft, velvety, slightly powdery leather note.

name: Caramel
description: Burnt sugar sweetness.

name: Coffee
description: Rich, roasted, bitter-sweet.

name: Honey
description: Sweet, warm, slightly animalic.

name: White Musk
description: Clean, soft, laundry-like musk.

name: Oakmoss
description: Earthy, damp forest floor character.

name: Agarwood
description: Synonym for oud. The infected heartwood of Aquilaria trees.

name: Pink Pepper
description: Lighter and fruitier than black pepper, with a rosy, bright spiciness.

name: Star Anise
description: Sweet, warm, licorice-like spice used in oriental compositions.

name: Elemi
description: Fresh, citrusy resin with a peppery, incense-like character.

name: Orris
description: The dried root of iris. Powdery, violet-like, earthy, and extremely precious.

name: Magnolia
description: Creamy, citrusy, slightly fruity white floral.

name: Neroli
description: Distilled from bitter orange blossoms. Fresh, green, slightly honeyed.

name: White Tea
description: Delicate, clean, slightly sweet and vegetal.

name: Black Tea
description: Smoky, slightly tannic, aromatic tea note.

name: Rum
description: Warm, sweet, boozy, with caramelized sugar character.

name: Whiskey
description: Smoky, oaky, warm boozy note with malty sweetness.

name: Dark Chocolate
description: Rich, bitter, roasted cacao with depth and warmth.

name: Almond
description: Sweet, nutty, marzipan-like note.

name: Pistachio
description: Creamy, green, nutty, and slightly sweet.

name: Birch
description: Smoky, leathery, tar-like woody note.

name: Moss
description: Damp, green, forest-floor character similar to oakmoss.

name: Sea Salt
description: Mineral, ozonic, marine note evoking ocean breeze.

name: Driftwood
description: Weathered, salty, dry woody note.

name: Pear
description: Crisp, juicy, slightly sweet green fruit.

name: Cherry
description: Sweet, tart, rich red fruit with almond undertones.

name: Blackcurrant
description: Tart, dark berry note with a slightly catty, green facet.

name: Violet
description: Powdery, sweet, slightly green floral with a candy-like character.

name: Green Tea
description: Clean, vegetal, slightly bitter and refreshing.

name: Lily
description: Fresh, clean, dewy white floral with green facets.

name: Olive
description: Green, slightly bitter, Mediterranean character.
